metrics.narrative: The business case is argued qualitatively (fixing a contractual process the buyer itself calls 'a mess') rather than with a hard ROI model for the core deal. The only quantified figures on record relate to option-sizing: Émeline Le Foyer estimated Publicis's addressable procurement spend at roughly €5bn (of ~€50bn total vendor/expense spend) to size the optional IAnalyze module (9 Jun 2026 email), and Zycus internally justified its discount partly on a 4x increase in licensed business users (Pierre's email, 29 Jun 2026). No quantified value case exists yet for the core iContract/iSupplier/iRequest scope itself.

champion.narrative: Émeline Le Foyer (Global IT Procurement Director, operational champion) drove weeks of active redlining and coordination (16, 20, 26, 29-30 Jun 2026 emails), pushed the NDA through in under a day ('Your AI agent is efficient! :) We'll route for signature shortly', 9 Jun 2026), and has been negotiating the Merlin for iContract module placement directly. The one real flag: as of the 8 Jul 2026 Next Step entry she is stepping back on leave, with a Publicis Re:Sources category manager covering commercial calls in her place through the final signing stretch — a continuity risk on an otherwise strong champion.

competition.narrative: No competitor or alternative vendor is named anywhere across the full 14-month relationship (first discovery call 27 May 2025 through today), and the CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 record explicitly logs 'No competition' as of 4 Jun 2026. Combined with the 'No RFP' decision-process note, this reads as a direct, relationship-led sole-source deal rather than a competitive evaluation.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated and specific, not generic: 'Actual contractual process is a mess / Email validation / No dashboard / No possibility to know easily what are the next steps' (CRM MEDDPICC 2.0, updated 4 Jun 2026). This has been the consistent framing across the deal's contracting phase and lines up with the products in scope (iContract, iRequest, iSupplier).

sources: Task email, Émeline Le Foyer, 9 Jun 2026
paper_process.narrative: The paper trail is far along: NDA executed (Émeline, 9 Jun 2026: 'We'll route for signature shortly'); MSA legal redlines resolved per Zycus's own email ('All legal clauses have now been resolved', 29 Jun 2026); OF1 finalized with a typo fix, formatting change, and Optional Support Services added (30 Jun 2026); and the SoW — the standard contracting choke point — was sent 9 Jul 2026 immediately after the 8 Jul contracting call. Separately, Publicis's own PASS sustainability questionnaire was completed by Zycus 2 Jul 2026, though a OneTrust vendor privacy declaration remained only 6/17 answered on Zycus's side as of 30 Jun 2026.

economic_buyer.narrative: Florence Tinsley-Roy, Chief Procurement Officer, is the confirmed Decision Maker on the Opportunity Contact Role and is named as economic buyer in the CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 record. She is not a passive sponsor: she personally set the closing deadline around her own schedule ('She wants to sign before end of July (She is in vacation in 2 weeks)', Next Step, 8 Jul 2026) and was on the 8 Jul 2026 contracting call herself.

decision_process.narrative: The buying committee is Florence (EB) plus Émeline Le Foyer (champion, day-to-day negotiation) plus a Publicis Re:Sources category manager handling commercial logistics, with Stephen Cook (process/tools) and Paras (external technical consultant) required to validate the SoW before it can be finalized. That validation thread went quiet from 12 Jun to 8 Jul 2026 despite four reminders, then reconvened on a call with Florence on 8 Jul 2026 — a real process gate that has just reopened rather than closed.

decision_criteria.narrative: There was no formal RFP or scored criteria matrix (CRM Decision Process: 'No RFP / Starting Contractual Discussion'); the practical criteria have been scope-fit questions worked through directly with Zycus: whether Merlin for iContract sits inside the core module or as a paid option, and whether the process/tools and technical stakeholders (Stephen Cook, Paras) are satisfied the platform matches Publicis's existing contractual process. The 4 Jun 2026 MEDDPICC update frames this exactly: 'ensure that Steffen and Paras are aligned and that Florence know enough existing process.'

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and moving: a buyer email arrived 8 Jul 2026, previously-dormant stakeholders rejoined the process the same day, and the final gating document (SoW) went out days later.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 7 May, 62 days old. Stage Vendor Selected entered 26 May (43 days in stage). Target signature date was 15 July per 24 June close plan, now 7 days overdue. Revised close plan from 8 July entry targets contract finalization 24 July and close 30 July (22 days out). Typical Vendor Selected to close is 30-45 days; at 43 days in stage the deal is within normal range but behind the buyer's own 15 July target. Forward slip: close date has held at 30 July since creation, but internal milestone (signature) has slipped from 15 July to implicit late-July. Required pace: must finalize MSA/OF1/OF2 and execute contracts in 22 days. SoW workshop (critical to OF2) was never confirmed after 4 reminders as of 12 June; if it has not occurred, OF2 cannot be finalized and the 24 July contract date is at risk. Most recent activity 13 July (5 days ago, within 14-day fresh window), but it is a rep-side Salesforce activity; last confirmed buyer two-way engagement was the 8 July call with Émeline and Florence references (confidence 90%, close plan reaffirmed). No forcing condition active (not cold, <3 overdue deliverables), but one contributor present: two key process stakeholders have been unresponsive for 26 days, blocking SoW finalization.
evidence: SF Next_Step__c 8 July entry: confidence 90%, Florence wants to sign before end of July, close plan includes SoW send 9 July, commercial call 9-10 July, Merlin demo 10 July, contract finalization 24 July
SF Next_Step__c 29 June entry: Émeline did not push back on Merlin for iContract as core (vs option), OF1 almost done, closing plan aligned for mid-July signature
SF Next_Step__c 24 June entry: BAFO sent 24 June, negotiation meeting 25 June, signature target 15 July, project start last week of July

SF Next_Step__c 12 June entry: Émeline (purchaser) very engaged (4 versions of OF1), legal on track, but Stephen Cook (Head of procurement performance) and Paras (external tech consultant) unresponsive after 4 reminders for SoW workshop; escalation to Florence planned if no answer by Monday afternoon
SF Next_Step__c 1 June entry: Florence confirmed as champion, willingness to close ASAP for quick win (go-live before end of FY26)
SF CloseDate 30 July 2026 (22 days out), stage Vendor Selected, forecast Commit, amount $250K
north_star_verdict.headline: 22 days to close, final contractual steps in flight, but process/tech stakeholders (Stephen Cook, Paras) have been dark for 26 days and SoW workshop was never confirmed; signature target was 15 July, now likely to slip unless contracts finalize by 24 July per latest close plan.

flags: Unblock silent process stakeholders (procurement performance lead and external tech consultant) who have not replied to 4 reminders as of 12 June; escalate directly to Florence (CPO, champion) to ensure their alignment does not block signature before 15 July.

Multi-thread and add missing buyer-side contact roles: Émeline Le Foyer (Global IT Procurement Director, driving OF1/OF2 iterations) and any other active stakeholders to the opportunity contact roles (currently only Florence is listed).

Multi-threading is thin: Florence (EB, champion) and Émeline (purchaser) are active, but Stephen Cook and Paras (critical process/tools and tech validation stakeholders) are unresponsive, and legal contact is unnamed; single-threading risk if Émeline or Florence become unavailable (Florence vacation imminent).

No quantified business value case (ROI, cost savings, cycle-time reduction) documented; pain is qualitative (procurement process inadequacy, urgency for quick win); quantifying the value case would strengthen EB/CFO sign-off and differentiation, especially with Merlin (agentic AI) positioning.
Technical validation incomplete: SoW workshop to finalize OF2 and validate assumptions never scheduled despite being critical path item in 21 May and 12 June close plans
No competitive intelligence (RFP context, shortlist rationale, sole-source confirmation) to inform positioning or last-minute competitor risk

Re-establish direct executive contact with Florence Tinsley-Roy (no confirmed direct touch since 1 Jun, 36 days) before signature to lock her decision on Merlin iContract scope and protect roughly $50K of TCV.

Escalate the stalled Stephen Cook / Paras thread now, per the 12 Jun close-plan's own contingency, since four reminders over 25 days have gone unanswered and the SoW/OF2 workshop is required before signature.

With signature target 7 days out (15 July) and Florence on vacation in two weeks per 8 July entry, executive alignment assumed rather than recently validated.

No competitive intelligence: no competitor documented in any Salesforce field or Next_Step entry despite Vendor Selected stage entry 26 May indicating competitive evaluation occurred. best_practice_check.summary: The deal's core sales work is essentially done; remaining leverage is in closing out the last validation gate and protecting continuity through the signing window.

customer_preference.evidence: Florence has driven the deal for 14 months and is personally setting the signing deadline around her own schedule ('She wants to sign before end of July (She is in vacation in 2 weeks)', Next_Step__c, 8 Jul 2026).

competitive_position.summary: No competitor documented in Salesforce or Next_Step__c trail. Deal is in Vendor Selected stage (entered 26 May), and BAFO was submitted 24 June, both of which imply a prior competitive evaluation, but no rival vendor is named. Likely sole-sourced or competition resolved pre-opportunity creation. Positioning: Zycus is the selected vendor for a multi-module CLM + supplier management solution (iContract, iSupplier, iRequest) with AI/Merlin capabilities (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA). Merlin for iContract is being positioned as a core differentiator (Merlin demo scheduled 10 July per 8 July Next_Step__c), though it was at risk of being downgraded to optional (29 June entry: could impact TCV by 50K). No documented competitive wedge because no documented competitor.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A Vendor Selected, Commit-forecast deal in its final contracting stretch: the economic buyer and champion are both engaged, the MSA/OF1/NDA are essentially done, and the SoW went out this week right after previously-stalled technical stakeholders rejoined the process. The main exposure is timeline compression, not deal quality.

deal_risk.text: Only 21 days remain to the 30 Jul 2026 close date, and the SoW, the demo, and the final contract package still need buyer sign-off in that window, while Florence herself becomes unavailable for two weeks in mid-July.

A Zycus-side compliance item (the OneTrust vendor privacy declaration, 6 of 17 questions answered as of 30 Jun 2026) was still open; if unresolved it could delay Publicis's own supplier/compliance clearance independent of the contract itself.

win_position.text: Florence Tinsley-Roy (CPO, confirmed Decision Maker/EB) has personally driven this deal for over a year and is setting the signing deadline herself, telling the team on 8 Jul 2026 she 'wants to sign before end of July.'

All commercial paper is essentially done: NDA executed (9 Jun 2026), MSA legal clauses resolved (29 Jun 2026), OF1 finalized with Optional Support Services added (30 Jun 2026), and the SoW sent following the 8 Jul 2026 contracting call.
No competitor has been named across 14+ months of engagement and the CRM MEDDPICC record explicitly states 'No competition' as of 4 Jun 2026; this is a relationship-led, sole-source close.

Stephen Cook and Paras (the technical/process validators for the SoW) had gone unresponsive since 12 Jun 2026 despite four reminders; they only rejoined on the 8 Jul 2026 call, so their sign-off on the newly-sent SoW is not yet confirmed.
Champion Émeline Le Foyer is stepping back from day-to-day commercial calls while on leave, with a Publicis Re:Sources category manager covering in her place during the final stretch to signature.

deal_momentum.text: Genuine two-way buyer engagement in the last 24-48h: a buyer email was received 8 Jul 2026 and the long-silent Stephen Cook/Paras thread reactivated the same day.

The SoW, the final gating document in the standard contracting sequence, was sent 9 Jul 2026 immediately after the 8 Jul 2026 call, and a Merlin for iContract AI-features demo is being scheduled for 10 or 13 Jul 2026.
The signature target has slipped from mid-June to 15 Jul to now 'before end of July' across three consecutive Next Step updates, so recent activity is real but the calendar has repeatedly moved.

customer_commitment.text: Florence is personally timing the deal around her own vacation schedule, and Émeline routed the NDA for signature within a day of receiving it (9 Jun 2026: 'we'll route for signature shortly').

Beyond Florence and Émeline, engagement from Stephen Cook and Paras was effectively dormant for nearly a month (12 Jun to 8 Jul 2026) despite repeated outreach, showing commitment is not yet even across the full buying committee.

• Qualified stage with proposal sent 1 May 2026 and target signature mid-July. 4 prior Avoma meetings (May/Sep 2025) with Cedric Elmaleh predate Florence's CPO arrival and are not directly relevant. The current cycle is partner-led (Bertrand intro) with no recorded Florence call yet on this specific opp.
• Florence's compelling event per Description is a year-end mandate to demonstrate procurement impact, not specifically AI; the deal is structured as a quick-win CLM/Intake replication of the PwC blueprint. Pushed UP from prior 5 to 6 based on the rich Description + Merlin in scope + named CPO champion with prior Zycus experience. Score 6 reflects Florence's potential AI championship without explicit AI-led mandate captured yet.

Multi-threading is thin: Florence (EB, champion) and Émeline (purchaser) are active, but Stephen Cook and Paras (critical process/tools and tech validation stakeholders) are unresponsive, and legal contact is unnamed; single-threading risk if Émeline or Florence become unavailable (Florence vacation imminent).

No quantified business value case (ROI, cost savings, cycle-time reduction) documented; pain is qualitative (procurement process inadequacy, urgency for quick win); quantifying the value case would strengthen EB/CFO sign-off and differentiation, especially with Merlin (agentic AI) positioning.
